problem.name: tsp_benchmark
problem.description: Euclidean TSP on the TSPLIB95 benchmark (known optima). The solver is run on several dev instances; score = mean gap %% above the known optimum (lower is better, 0 = optimal on every instance). Distances use the TSPLIB metric: each edge rounded to the nearest integer. The winning solver is re-evaluated on held-out instances at the end.
problem.stats: TSPLIB95 dev set: berlin52 (52 cities), st70 (70 cities), eil76 (76 cities), kroA100 (100 cities), rd100 (100 cities). Optima are known; score is the mean gap %% above optimum across all dev instances (TSPLIB rounded-integer metric). 6 held-out instances are kept hidden for final verification.

payload.system_prompt: You are the Strategist of an autonomous research lab. You propose diverse, testable algorithmic hypotheses. Each must be meaningfully different from the others. Respond with a single JSON object only.

hypotheses.name: Greedy Init + Or-opt + 2-opt
hypotheses.strategy: Greedy construction with Or-opt and 2-opt local search
hypotheses.hypothesis: Building the initial tour greedily (always adding the shortest available edge that doesn't violate tour constraints) and then applying Or-opt (1-, 2-, 3-city segment relocations) followed by 2-opt will achieve a mean gap below 5.4%, because greedy construction yields a better starting point than nearest-neighbor (~15-20% closer to optimum) and Or-opt escapes local optima that 2-opt cannot resolve by moving segments rather than reversing edges.
hypotheses.risk: Or-opt and 2-opt may still get stuck in the same local optima on some instances, and the greedy construction overhead could consume too much of the 10s budget for larger instances if not implemented efficiently.

hypotheses.name: Multi-restart 2-opt with Random Perturbation
hypotheses.strategy: Iterated Local Search with random restarts
hypotheses.hypothesis: Running nearest-neighbor construction from multiple random starting cities, applying full 2-opt to each, and keeping the best solution will beat the single-start baseline below 5.4% mean gap, because the 52-100 city instances are small enough to complete 10-20 restarts within 10 seconds and diversity in starting tours prevents convergence to a single poor local optimum.
hypotheses.risk: All random restarts may converge to similar local optima if the 2-opt landscape has few basins of attraction, yielding diminishing returns; the time budget may also be tighter than expected if 2-opt is slow in pure Python.

hypotheses.name: Lin-Kernighan Style 3-opt
hypotheses.hypothesis: Implementing sequential 3-opt moves (examining all triple-edge exchanges) with a candidate neighbor list restricted to the 10 nearest neighbors per city will achieve a mean gap below 3% because 3-opt can escape 2-opt local optima and the neighbor-list restriction keeps complexity manageable within 10 seconds for instances under 100 cities.
hypotheses.risk: Pure Python 3-opt over all city triples is O(n^3) per pass and may be too slow even with neighbor lists; incorrect segment reversal logic in 3-opt reconnection cases is a common implementation bug that could produce invalid tours.

hypotheses.name: Iterated Or-opt with Double-Bridge Perturbation
hypotheses.strategy: Iterated Local Search with double-bridge kick
hypotheses.hypothesis: Alternating between full Or-opt local search and a double-bridge perturbation (a 4-opt move that splits the tour into 4 segments and reconnects them in a different order) will achieve a mean gap below 4% because double-bridge moves are non-sequential and escape all 2-opt and 3-opt local optima, and the small instance sizes allow many ILS iterations within 10 seconds, enabling broad exploration of the tour space.
hypotheses.risk: Double-bridge perturbation is random and may occasionally produce very poor tours that waste ILS iterations; without a good acceptance criterion the algorithm may not reliably improve over a well-tuned Or-opt run alone.

```python
import time
import math
import random

def solve(cities):
    t0 = time.time()
    budget = 8.5
    n = len(cities)
    
    if n == 1:
        return [0]
    if n == 2:
        return [0, 1]
    
    # Precompute distances
    def dist(i, j):
        dx = cities[i][0] - cities[j][0]
        dy = cities[i][1] - cities[j][1]
        return int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)
    
    # Distance matrix
    d = [[0]*n for _ in range(n)]
    for i in range(n):
        for j in range(n):
            if i != j:
                d[i][j] = dist(i, j)
    
    # Nearest neighbor construction
    def nn_tour(start):
        visited = [False]*n
        tour = [start]
        visited[start] = True
        for _ in range(n-1):
            cur = tour[-1]
            best_d = float('inf')
            best_c = -1
            for j in range(n):
                if not visited[j] and d[cur][j] < best_d:
                    best_d = d[cur][j]
                    best_c = j
            tour.append(best_c)
            visited[best_c] = True
        return tour
    
    def tour_length(tour):
        return sum(d[tour[i]][tour[(i+1)%n]] for i in range(n))
    
    # Greedy edge construction
    def greedy_tour():
        edges = []
        for i in range(n):
            for j in range(i+1, n):
                edges.append((d[i][j], i, j))
        edges.sort()
        degree = [0]*n
        adj = [[] for _ in range(n)]
        # Union-Find for cycle detection
        parent = list(range(n))
        def find(x):
            while parent[x] != x:
                parent[x] = parent[parent[x]]
                x = parent[x]
            return x
        def union(x, y):
            px, py = find(x), find(y)
            if px == py:
                return False
            parent[px] = py
            return True
        
        edge_count = 0
        for cost, i, j in edges:
            if edge_count == n:
                break
            if degree[i] >= 2 or degree[j] >= 2:
                continue
            # Check if adding creates premature cycle
            if edge_count < n-1 and find(i) == find(j):
                continue
            union(i, j)
            adj[i].append(j)
            adj[j].append(i)
            degree[i] += 1
            degree[j] += 1
            edge_count += 1
        
        if edge_count < n:
            return nn_tour(0)
        
        # Build tour from adjacency
        tour = []
        visited = [False]*n
        # Find a starting node
        start = 0
        cur = start
        prev = -1
        for _ in range(n):
            tour.append(cur)
            visited[cur] = True
            moved = False
            for nxt in adj[cur]:
                if nxt != prev and not visited[nxt]:
                    prev = cur
                    cur = nxt
                    moved = True
                    break
            if not moved:
                break
        
        if len(tour) != n:
            return nn_tour(0)
        return tour
    
    # 2-opt
    def two_opt(tour):
        improved = True
        dnl = [False]*n  # don't-look bits
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for i in range(n):
                if dnl[i]:
                    continue
                found = False
                i1 = (i+1) % n
                for j in range(i+2, n):
                    if j == n-1 and i == 0:
                        continue
                    j1 = (j+1) % n
                    gain = (d[tour[i]][tour[i1]] + d[tour[j]][tour[j1]] -
                            d[tour[i]][tour[j]] - d[tour[i1]][tour[j1]])
                    if gain > 0:
                        tour[i1:j+1] = tour[i1:j+1][::-1]
                        improved = True
                        found = True
                        dnl[i] = False
                        dnl[j] = False
                if not found:
                    dnl[i] = True
            if time.time() - t0 > budget:
                break
        return tour
    
    # Or-opt: move segments of length seg_len
    def or_opt(tour, seg_len):
        improved = True
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for i in range(n):
                if time.time() - t0 > budget:
                    return tour
                # Segment: tour[i], tour[i+1], ..., tour[i+seg_len-1]
                a = tour[(i-1) % n]
                seg = [tour[(i+k) % n] for k in range(seg_len)]
                b = tour[(i+seg_len) % n]
                
                # Cost of removing segment
                remove_gain = d[a][seg[0]] + d[seg[-1]][b] - d[a][b]
                
                # Try inserting after each other position
                for j in range(n):
                    # Skip positions within or adjacent to segment
                    skip = False
                    for k in range(-1, seg_len+1):
                        if j == (i+k) % n:
                            skip = True
                            break
                    if skip:
                        continue
                    
                    c = tour[j]
                    c1 = tour[(j+1) % n]
                    
                    insert_cost = d[c][seg[0]] + d[seg[-1]][c1] - d[c][c1]
                    
                    if remove_gain - insert_cost > 0:
                        # Perform the move
                        # Rebuild tour
                        new_tour = []
                        idx = set((i+k)%n for k in range(seg_len))
                        # Build tour without segment, then insert after j
                        temp = []
                        for k in range(n):
                            if k not in idx:
                                temp.append(tour[k])
                        # Find position of j in temp
                        # j is the position before insertion in original
                        # c = tour[j], find c in temp
                        pos = temp.index(c)
                        new_tour = temp[:pos+1] + seg + temp[pos+1:]
                        if len(new_tour) == n:
                            tour[:] = new_tour
                            improved = True
                            break
            if time.time() - t0 > budget:
                break
        return tour
    
    # Or-opt with reversed segment
    def or_opt_rev(tour, seg_len):
        improved = True
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for i in range(n):
                if time.time() - t0 > budget:
                    return tour
                a = tour[(i-1) % n]
                seg = [tour[(i+k) % n] for k in range(seg_len)]
                b = tour[(i+seg_len) % n]
                
                remove_gain = d[a][seg[0]] + d[seg[-1]][b] - d[a][b]
                seg_rev = seg[::-1]
                
                for j in range(n):
                    skip = False
                    for k in range(-1, seg_len+1):
                        if j == (i+k) % n:
                            skip = True
                            break
                    if skip:
                        continue
                    
                    c = tour[j]
                    c1 = tour[(j+1) % n]
                    
                    # Try both orientations
                    insert_cost_fwd = d[c][seg[0]] + d[seg[-1]][c1] - d[c][c1]
                    insert_cost_rev = d[c][seg_rev[0]] + d[seg_rev[-1]][c1] - d[c][c1]
                    
                    best_ins = max(remove_gain - insert_cost_fwd, remove_gain - insert_cost_rev)
                    if best_ins > 0:
                        use_seg = seg if (remove_gain - insert_cost_fwd >= remove_gain - insert_cost_rev) else seg_rev
                        idx = set((i+k)%n for k in range(seg_len))
                        temp = []
                        for k in range(n):
                            if k not in idx:
                                temp.append(tour[k])
                        pos = temp.index(c)
                        new_tour = temp[:pos+1] + use_seg + temp[pos+1:]
                        if len(new_tour) == n:
                            tour[:] = new_tour
                            improved = True
                            break
            if time.time() - t0 > budget:
                break
        return tour
    
    # Build initial tour
    best_tour = greedy_tour()
    best_len = tour_length(best_tour)
    
    # Try NN from multiple starts
    for start in range(min(n, 10)):
        if time.time() - t0 > budget * 0.3:
            break
        t = nn_tour(start)
        tl = tour_length(t)
        if tl < best_len:
            best_len = tl
            best_tour = t[:]
    
    # Apply 2-opt
    best_tour = two_opt(best_tour[:])
    best_len = tour_length(best_tour)
    
    # Apply Or-opt
    for seg in [1, 2, 3]:
        if time.time() - t0 > budget:
            break
        best_tour = or_opt_rev(best_tour, seg)
    
    best_tour = two_opt(best_tour)
    best_len = tour_length(best_tour)
    
    # Iterated improvement with perturbation
    current_tour = best_tour[:]
    current_len = best_len
    
    while time.time() - t0 < budget:
        # Double-bridge perturbation
        def double_bridge(tour):
            pos = sorted(random.sample(range(1, n), 4))
            a, b, c, d_ = pos
            # Split into 4 segments and reconnect differently
            seg1 = tour[:a]
            seg2 = tour[a:b]
            seg3 = tour[b:c]
            seg4 = tour[c:]
            # One of the non-sequential reconnections
            new_tour = seg1 + seg3 + seg2 + seg4
            return new_tour
        
        new_tour = double_bridge(current_tour[:])
        new_tour = two_opt(new_tour)
        for seg in [1, 2, 3]:
            if time.time() - t0 > budget:
                break
            new_tour = or_opt_rev(new_tour, seg)
        if time.time() - t0 < budget:
            new_tour = two_opt(new_tour)
        
        new_len = tour_length(new_tour)
        if new_len < best_len:
            best_len = new_len
            best_tour = new_tour[:]
        
        # Accept if better (greedy acceptance)
        if new_len < current_len:
            current_len = new_len
            current_tour = new_tour[:]
        else:
            # Reset to best
            current_tour = best_tour[:]
            current_len = best_len
    
    return best_tour
```

                        # Reverse segment between i+1 and j

def solve(cities):

    if n == 1:

    if n == 2:

        dy = cities[a][1] - cities[b][1]
        return math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy)

    def rdist(a, b):

        dy = cities[a][1] - cities[b][1]
        return int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)

    # Precompute distance matrix
    D = [[0]*n for _ in range(n)]
    for i in range(n):
        for j in range(n):
            if i != j:
                D[i][j] = rdist(i, j)

    # Candidate lists: 10 nearest neighbors
    K = min(10, n-1)
    neighbors = []
    for i in range(n):
        dists = sorted(range(n), key=lambda j: D[i][j] if j != i else float('inf'))
        neighbors.append(dists[:K])

    def tour_length(tour):
        return sum(D[tour[i]][tour[(i+1)%n]] for i in range(n))

    def nn_tour(start):
        visited = [False]*n
        tour = [start]

                    best_d = D[cur][nb]
                    best_next = nb
            if best_next == -1:

                        best_d = D[cur][j]
                        best_next = j
            tour.append(best_next)
            visited[best_next] = True

        improved = True
        pos = [0]*n

            improved = False

                    # Try reversing segment between i+1 and j

                        # candidate list is sorted, so if this is worse, skip

                    i1 = (i+1)%n

                            tour[i1:] = seg[:l1]

                        dnb[cj] = False

import time

    budget = 8.5

        dy = cities[i][1] - cities[j][1]
        return int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)

    # Precompute distance matrix
    D = [[0]*n for _ in range(n)]
    for i in range(n):
        for j in range(n):
            D[i][j] = dist(i, j)

    # Nearest neighbor lists
    K = min(15, n-1)
    neighbors = []
    for i in range(n):
        dists = sorted(range(n), key=lambda j: D[i][j])
        neighbors.append([j for j in dists if j != i][:K])

    def tour_length(tour):
        return sum(D[tour[i]][tour[(i+1)%n]] for i in range(n))

    def nn_tour(start):
        visited = [False]*n
        tour = [start]
        visited[start] = True
        for _ in range(n-1):
            cur = tour[-1]
            best_next = -1
            best_d = float('inf')
            for j in neighbors[cur]:
                if not visited[j] and D[cur][j] < best_d:
                    best_d = D[cur][j]
                    best_next = j
            if best_next == -1:
                for j in range(n):
                    if not visited[j] and D[cur][j] < best_d:
                        best_d = D[cur][j]
                        best_next = j
            tour.append(best_next)
            visited[best_next] = True
        return tour

    def two_opt(tour):
        improved = True
        pos = [0]*n
        for i,c in enumerate(tour): pos[c] = i
        while improved:
            if time.time()-t0 > budget: break
            improved = False
            for i in range(n):
                ci = tour[i]
                ci1 = tour[(i+1)%n]
                d_ci = D[ci][ci1]
                for cj in neighbors[ci]:
                    j = pos[cj]
                    cj1 = tour[(j+1)%n]
                    if cj == ci1 or cj1 == ci: continue
                    gain = d_ci + D[cj][cj1] - D[ci][cj] - D[ci1][cj1]
                    if gain > 0:
                        # Reverse segment between i+1 and j
                        a, b = (i+1)%n, j
                        if a <= b:
                            seg = tour[a:b+1]
                            seg.reverse()
                            tour[a:b+1] = seg
                        else:

                            seg = tour[a:] + tour[:b+1]
                            seg.reverse()
                            l1 = n - a
                            tour[a:] = seg[:l1]
                            tour[:b+1] = seg[l1:]
                        for k2 in range(n): pos[tour[k2]] = k2
                        improved = True
                        break
        return tour

    def or_opt_move(tour, seg_len):
        pos = [0]*n
        for i,c in enumerate(tour): pos[c] = i

            if time.time()-t0 > budget: break
            improved = False
            for idx in range(n):

                i0 = idx
                i1 = (idx + seg_len - 1) % n
                prev_i = (i0 - 1) % n
                next_i1 = (i1 + 1) % n

                c_prev = tour[prev_i]
                c0 = tour[i0]
                c1 = tour[i1]
                c_next = tour[next_i1]

                if c_prev == c_next: continue  # seg_len == n

                old_cost = D[c_prev][c0] + D[c1][c_next]
                new_link = D[c_prev][c_next]

                            break

                    if cj == c_prev: continue

                    insert_cost = D[cj][c0] + D[c1][cj_next] - D[cj][cj_next]
                    if removal_gain - insert_cost > 0:
                        # Remove segment and reinsert
                        # Build new tour
                        seg = []
                        cur = i0
                        for _ in range(seg_len):
                            seg.append(tour[cur])
                            cur = (cur+1)%n

                        new_tour = []
                        cur = next_i1
                        for _ in range(n - seg_len):
                            new_tour.append(tour[cur])

                        pos2 = {c: k for k,c in enumerate(new_tour)}

                        tour[:] = new_tour
                        for k2 in range(n): pos[tour[k2]] = k2
                        improved = True
                        break
        return tour

        tour = two_opt(tour)
        if time.time()-t0 > budget: return tour
        tour = or_opt_move(tour, 1)
        if time.time()-t0 > budget: return tour
        tour = or_opt_move(tour, 2)
        if time.time()-t0 > budget: return tour
        tour = or_opt_move(tour, 3)
        if time.time()-t0 > budget: return tour
        tour = two_opt(tour)
        return tour

        if l < best_len:
            best_len = l
            best_tour = t[:]

    # ILS loop
    current_tour = best_tour[:]
    current_len = best_len

    while time.time()-t0 < budget:
        # Perturbation
        new_tour = double_bridge(current_tour[:])
        new_tour = local_search(new_tour)
        if time.time()-t0 > budget:
            nl = tour_length(new_tour)
            if nl < best_len:
                best_len = nl
                best_tour = new_tour[:]
            break
        new_len = tour_length(new_tour)
        # Accept if better (or with small probability for diversification)
        if new_len < current_len:

            current_len = new_len
            if new_len < best_len:
                best_len = new_len
                best_tour = new_tour[:]
        else:
            # Restart from best with some probability
            if random.random() < 0.3:
                current_tour = best_tour[:]
                current_len = best_len

    return best_tour
